channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: At least 58 dead and 160 injured in a roof collapse at a nightclub in the Dominican Republic
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — At least 58 people died and 160 others were injured in the Dominican capital early Tuesday when the roof collapsed at an iconic nightclub where politicians, athletes and others were attending a merengue concert, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump administration says New York must end Manhattan driving tolls this month
item.description: The Trump administration said Tuesday that it's not backing off its latest deadline for New York to end its $9 congestion toll on drivers entering the most traffic-snarled parts of Manhattan, despite a recent court filing that indicated the charge would remain at least through the summer. U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Army to begin limited use of horse-drawn caissons for Arlington National Cemetery funerals
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Army will soon begin limited use of horse-drawn caissons for funerals at Arlington National Cemetery, phasing in the long-held tradition after nearly two years of work to improve the care of the horses.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Immigration judge to rule Friday on possible release of detained Columbia activist Mahmoud Khalil
item.description: JENA, La. (AP) — An immigration judge in Louisiana said she will decide later this week whether the government can continue to detain Mahmoud Khalil, the Columbia University activist facing deportation for his role in pro-Palestinian campus protests.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: A Florida man faces execution for killing a Miami Herald employee abducted on her lunch break
item.description: STARKE, Fla. (AP) — A man convicted of killing a woman who was carjacked on her lunch break from her job at The Miami Herald is set to be executed Tuesday evening. Michael Tanzi was scheduled to receive a lethal injection at Florida State Prison for the 2000 abduction and strangling of Janet Acosta.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Huntsman Cancer Institute breaks ground on new $400M Utah County cancer center
item.description: The Huntsman Cancer Institute broke ground on its new location in Utah County. Here's the project details and timeline.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: Keystone pipeline shut down after North Dakota rupture
item.description: The affected segment has been isolated, and operations and containment resources have been mobilized to site,” the company said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Rapper 'Fat Flow' gets 33 years for kidnapping migrants
item.description: A judge has sentenced a rapper calling himself Fat Flow to 33 years and four months in prison for the 2023 kidnapping of seven migrants in Juarez, Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, KTLA 5

item.title: Democratic-led cities and states push back on threats to cut US school funding over DEI
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Some Democratic-led states and cities are pushing back on a Trump administration threat to cut education funding over diversity, equity and inclusion programs, creating a standoff that could test how far the White House is willing to go to press its demands on the nation’s schools.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hawaii's Kilauea volcano spews fountains of lava in latest eruption episode
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Scientists were bracing for high lava fountains Tuesday during the latest episode of an ongoing eruption of Hawaii's Kilauea volcano.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Internal Revenue Service agrees to send immigrant tax data to ICE for enforcement
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Internal Revenue Service has agreed to share immigrants' tax data with Immigration and Customs Enforcement for the purpose of identifying and deporting people illegally in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump administration cancels $188M in grants New York City was using to shelter migrants
item.description: The Trump administration canceled $188 million in federal grants meant to reimburse New York City for sheltering migrants, saying the money was being spent to support illegal immigration and leading the city's mayor to vow to fight the clawback.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: White House insists Trump won't budge on 104 percent China tariff
item.description: The White House on Tuesday insisted that President Trump won’t back down on his threat to impose a more than 100 percent tariff rate on China, significantly escalating the trade war between the two nations hours before the deadline approaches.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump touts Supreme Court deportation ruling as a major victory, but legal fight is far from over
item.description: The Trump administration is touting a Supreme Court ruling allowing it to resume deportations under the Alien Enemies Act as a major victory, but the immigration fight is far from over.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst And Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Waiver allows Trump administration to bypass environmental rules to build more border walls
item.description: A waiver issued by the Department of Homeland Security allows the federal government to bypass environmental regulations and begin construction immediately on stretches of the border wall in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump to host El Salvador's president amid US deportation efforts
item.description: President Trump will welcome the leader of El Salvador at the White House next week to tout cooperation between the two nations as the U.S. sends deported migrants they claim are gang members to a massive El Salvadoran prison.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stephen A. Smith 'leaving all doors open' for presidential bid
item.description: Smith has a longstanding history of weighing in on politics, but has been particularly critical of the Democratic Party in the wake of President Trump’s success in the 2024 election, pushing for it to refocus on top issues for voters in last year’s election cycle including immigration and inflation.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: House Democrats targeting 35 Republicans in 2026
item.description: Last month, the National Republican Congressional Campaign Committee (NRCC) released its target list, which included 26 House Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court rules in Trump's favor on deportations and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WATCH: Dire wolf puppies howl for the first time in over 10,000 years
item.description: The dire wolf, who has been extinct for over 10,000 years was brought back to life as the world's first de-extinct animal.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: RFK Jr. plans to tell CDC to stop recommending fluoride in drinking water
item.description: The addition of low levels of fluoride to drinking water was long considered one of the greatest public health achievements of the last century.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: EPA says it will reconsider safety of fluoride in drinking water
item.description: Fluoride is intentionally added to drinking water to prevent tooth decay. About 200 million Americans drink water with added fluoride.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Florida charter captain Brock Horner ordered to have no contact with fisherman after arrest
item.description: A Florida charter captain whose angry exchange with a fisherman went viral has been ordered not to contact the alleged victim, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, NewsNation

item.title: Fan-favorite Krispy Kreme item returning for Easter
item.description: Krispy Kreme is celebrating Easter with an all-new doughnut collection, which includes a fan-favorite item. Starting Monday, fans can enjoy the all-new Hoppy Easter Collection, featuring three festive doughnuts and the strawberry doughnut.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Chief Justice Roberts pauses order to return Maryland dad mistakenly deported to El Salvador
item.description: Chief Justice John Roberts agreed Monday to pause a midnight deadline for the Trump administration to return a Maryland man mistakenly deported to a notorious prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Judge dismisses former Mississippi governor's defamation lawsuit against news outlet
item.description: JACKSON, Miss. (AP) — A judge has dismissed a lawsuit by former Mississippi Gov. Phil Bryant that claimed a local news outlet defamed him in public comments about its Pulitzer Prize-winning reporting on the misspending of $77 million in federal welfare funds.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump threatens additional 50 percent tariff on China
item.description: The White House had already imposed a 20 percent tariff on Chinese imports over fentanyl concerns, meaning China could face an effective tariff rate as high as 70 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: The Latest: Stocks sink globally as Trump pushes ahead with trade war
item.description: U.S. stocks are falling in a manic Monday after President Donald Trump doubled down on his tariffs, despite seeing how much financial markets would love to see him do the opposite.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Buying Bitcoin? Here's what to know, according to the Utah Dept. of Commerce
item.description: If you're considering investing in cryptocurrency, the Utah Dept. of Commerce has some advice to help you avoid becoming victim of a scam.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration asks Supreme Court to block order to return Maryland man from El Salvador
item.description: The Trump administration on Monday asked the Supreme Court to block a court order requiring it to return a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported to a notorious prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump, Netanyahu nix White House press conference
item.description: The White House on Monday opted to nix a formal press conference that would have had President Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu taking questions from a wider swath of reporters, choosing instead for the two leaders to face the small group of media in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: YPD: Man locks himself in store overnight, hauls items to door, calls 911 to get let out
item.description: Aaron Jones, 30, was booked into the Mahoning County jail on a charge of breaking and entering, a fifth-degree felony.
item.dc:creator: Joe Gorman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US stocks dip after careening through a manic day following Trump's latest tariff threat
item.description: U.S. stocks swung wildly in a manic Monday after President Donald Trump doubled down on his tariffs, despite seeing how much Wall Street wants him to do the opposite.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: RFK Jr. says MMR vaccine 'most effective' way to stop spread of measles after Texas child's death
item.description: Kennedy, who has a history of vaccine skepticism, made the remarks after visiting the family of an unvaccinated 8-year-old girl who died from the disease.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Jay North, TV's Dennis the Menace, dies at 73
item.description: Jay North, who starred as the towheaded mischief maker on TV's “Dennis the Menace” for four seasons starting in 1959, has died. He was 73.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Second child died from measles-related causes in West Texas
item.description: A second school-aged child in West Texas has died from a measles-related illness, a hospital spokesman confirmed Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Researchers developing at-home smell test to detect Alzheimer’s
item.description: A smell test is less invasive and more accessible than other methods like PET scans, lumbar punctures or blood tests.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5
